description:
You are assisting a developer who is tidying up some project data.  
Inside the workspace there is a directory:

/home/user/project_data

which already contains two data files:

1. /home/user/project_data/employees.csv  
2. /home/user/project_data/statuses.json  

Your job is to create a processed output directory, consolidate the information that is spread across the two source files, apply a series of filters, and generate three new files whose exact paths, names and formats are described below.

A. Pre-existing source files  
   1. /home/user/project_data/employees.csv  
      - CSV, comma-separated, UTF-8, with the following 6 lines (including header):  
        id,name,dept,salary  
        101,Alice Smith,Engineering,90000  
        102,Bob Johnson,Marketing,65000  
        103,Carol Martinez,Engineering,72000  
        104,David Lee,Engineering,68000  
        105,Eve Turner,HR,70000  
        106,Frank Wright,Engineering,80000  

   2. /home/user/project_data/statuses.json  
      - Valid JSON array, pretty-printed exactly like this:  
        [  
          {"id": 101, "active": true},  
          {"id": 102, "active": false},  
          {"id": 103, "active": true},  
          {"id": 104, "active": false},  
          {"id": 105, "active": true},  
          {"id": 106, "active": true}  
        ]  

B. What you must create  
   1. Make a new sub-directory named processed directly under /home/user/project_data  
      Path: /home/user/project_data/processed  
   2. Inside that directory generate *exactly* these three new files:  
      a) /home/user/project_data/processed/combined_report.csv  
      b) /home/user/project_data/processed/combined_report.json  
      c) /home/user/project_data/processed/processing.log  

C. Logic that your script/commands must implement  
   1. Read the two source datasets.  
   2. Perform an inner join on the numeric field id so that each employee record gains its corresponding “active” value.  
   3. Apply all of these filters simultaneously:  
      • active must be true  
      • dept must equal the literal string Engineering (case-sensitive)  
      • salary must be greater than or equal to 75000  
   4. Sort the remaining records by salary in descending (high-to-low) order.  
   5. Produce the CSV and JSON output files that contain only the surviving records, in the sorted order, following the exact formats in section D.  
   6. Produce a plain-text log file summarising each major step of your data-processing pipeline, using the wording in section D verbatim.  

D. Exact required contents of the new files (the automated grader will compare byte-for-byte):

1. /home/user/project_data/processed/combined_report.csv  
   (7 lines including header, UNIX line-endings LF)  
   id,name,dept,salary,active  
   101,Alice Smith,Engineering,90000,true  
   106,Frank Wright,Engineering,80000,true  

2. /home/user/project_data/processed/combined_report.json  
   A JSON array, pretty-printed with two‐space indentation and newline at EOF, exactly:  
   [  
     {  
       "id": 101,  
       "name": "Alice Smith",  
       "dept": "Engineering",  
       "salary": 90000,  
       "active": true  
     },  
     {  
       "id": 106,  
       "name": "Frank Wright",  
       "dept": "Engineering",  
       "salary": 80000,  
       "active": true  
     }  
   ]  

3. /home/user/project_data/processed/processing.log  
   The file must contain these 7 lines in precisely this order (each terminated with a single LF):  
   STEP 1: Loaded employees.csv (6 records)  
   STEP 2: Loaded statuses.json (6 records)  
   STEP 3: Performed inner join on 'id' (6 merged records)  
   STEP 4: Applied filters (active == true, dept == 'Engineering', salary >= 75000) (2 records remain)  
   STEP 5: Sorted by salary desc  
   STEP 6: Wrote /home/user/project_data/processed/combined_report.csv  
   STEP 7: Wrote /home/user/project_data/processed/combined_report.json  

E. Success criteria  
   • The directory /home/user/project_data/processed exists.  
   • The three files described above exist at the specified paths.  
   • Their byte-wise contents match exactly what is shown in section D.  
   • File permissions can be the default ones created by your shell tools; no special modes needed.  

The automated test harness will confirm every one of the points in section E, so ensure that your commands leave the system in that final state. You may use any standard shell utilities (e.g., awk, jq, csvkit, Python, etc.) available in a typical Ubuntu environment—how you accomplish the task is up to you, as long as the output strictly matches the required content.
truth:
Initial state (before the agent starts):
1. /home/user/project_data directory exists.
2. /home/user/project_data/employees.csv contains exactly:
id,name,dept,salary
101,Alice Smith,Engineering,90000
102,Bob Johnson,Marketing,65000
103,Carol Martinez,Engineering,72000
104,David Lee,Engineering,68000
105,Eve Turner,HR,70000
106,Frank Wright,Engineering,80000

3. /home/user/project_data/statuses.json contains exactly:
[
  {"id": 101, "active": true},
  {"id": 102, "active": false},
  {"id": 103, "active": true},
  {"id": 104, "active": false},
  {"id": 105, "active": true},
  {"id": 106, "active": true}
]

Expected final state after successful task completion:
1. /home/user/project_data/processed directory exists.
2. /home/user/project_data/processed/combined_report.csv contains:
id,name,dept,salary,active
101,Alice Smith,Engineering,90000,true
106,Frank Wright,Engineering,80000,true

3. /home/user/project_data/processed/combined_report.json contains:
[
  {
    "id": 101,
    "name": "Alice Smith",
    "dept": "Engineering",
    "salary": 90000,
    "active": true
  },
  {
    "id": 106,
    "name": "Frank Wright",
    "dept": "Engineering",
    "salary": 80000,
    "active": true
  }
]

4. /home/user/project_data/processed/processing.log contains:
STEP 1: Loaded employees.csv (6 records)
STEP 2: Loaded statuses.json (6 records)
STEP 3: Performed inner join on 'id' (6 merged records)
STEP 4: Applied filters (active == true, dept == 'Engineering', salary >= 75000) (2 records remain)
STEP 5: Sorted by salary desc
STEP 6: Wrote /home/user/project_data/processed/combined_report.csv
STEP 7: Wrote /home/user/project_data/processed/combined_report.json
